input_row.title: Ensure 'Minimum Inbound TLS Version' is set to '1.2' or higher
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, click the drop-down next to Minimum Inbound TLS Version and select 1.2 or higher. 5. Click Save. 6. Click Continue. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each function app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each function app requiring remediation, run the following command to update the TLS version: az functionapp config set --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name> --min-tls-version <1.2|1.3> Default Value: By default, TLS version is set to 1.2. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/configure-ssl-bindings#how- can-i-change-the-minimum-tls-versions-for-the-app 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-data- protection#dp-3-encrypt-sensitive-data-in-transit 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-network- security#ns-8-detect-and-disable-insecure-services-and-protocols 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.functions/get- azfunctionapp 6. https://csrc.nist.gov/news/2019/nist-publishes-sp-800-52-revision-2 CIS Controls:
input_row.reference: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/configure-ssl-bindings#how- can-i-change-the-minimum-tls-versions-for-the-app 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-data- protection#dp-3-encrypt-sensitive-data-in-transit 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-network- security#ns-8-detect-and-disable-insecure-services-and-protocols 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.functions/get- azfunctionapp 6. https://csrc.nist.gov/news/2019/nist-publishes-sp-800-52-revision-2 CIS Controls: Controls Version Control IG 1 IG 2 IG 3 v8 3.10 Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH). ● ● v7 14.4 Encrypt All Sensitive Information in Transit Encrypt all sensitive information in transit.  ● ●

first_opinion.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control specifies how to configure the 'Minimum Inbound TLS Version' which can be audited through Azure APIs.
- The remediation section includes Azure CLI commands for updating the TLS version which indicates that the control settings are accessible via API.
- Since the minimum TLS version is a standard configuration property in Azure Function Apps, it can be queried and validated through Azure Resource Manager APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_functionapp_tls_minimum_version_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the following:
- Use the Azure Resource Manager (ARM) API or Azure CLI commands to enumerate all Azure Function Apps within specified resource groups or subscriptions.
- The specific properties/settings to validate include the property related to 'minTlsVersion' for each function app.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria: 
  - **Pass**: The 'minTlsVersion' property is set to '1.2' or higher.
  - **Fail**: The 'minTlsVersion' property is set below '1.2'.
- Example API endpoint to retrieve configurations:
  ```
  GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/{functionAppName}?api-version=2021-02-01
  ```
- Example Azure CLI command to verify:
  ```
  az functionapp show --name <function-app-name> --resource-group <resource-group-name> --query minTlsVersion
  ```
This command will return the current minimum TLS version setting, which can then be compared against the required standard.

final_decision.response:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that the audit approach should be automated, as the 'Minimum Inbound TLS Version' setting for Azure Function Apps can be checked and validated using Azure Resource Manager APIs and Azure CLI. The properties are accessible through standard Azure resource APIs, making automation feasible and efficient.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
azure_functionapp_tls_minimum_version_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **Azure Resource/Service:** Azure Function Apps
- **API/CLI Method:** Use Azure Resource Manager (ARM) API or Azure CLI command `az functionapp config show`
- **Properties to Check:** `minTlsVersion` property in the configuration settings
- **Pass Criteria:** The `minTlsVersion` property is set to '1.2' or higher
- **Fail Criteria:** The `minTlsVersion` property is set to a version lower than '1.2'

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported the automated approach based on the accessibility of the required properties through Azure APIs.
